description: AviTag — a 15-aa synthetic biotin acceptor peptide (GLNDIFEAQKIEWHE) that is site-specifically biotinylated on Lys11 (K10 in original numbering) by the biotin ligase BirA in vivo or in vitro.

references.title: Use of peptide libraries to map the substrate specificity of a peptide-modifying enzyme: a 13 residue consensus peptide specifies biotinylation in Escherichia coli

references.authors: Beckett D, Kovaleva E, Schatz PJ
references.title: A minimal peptide substrate in biotin holoenzyme synthetase-catalyzed biotinylation

references.authors: Duffy S, Tsao KL, Waugh DS
references.title: Site-specific, enzymatic biotinylation of recombinant proteins in Spodoptera frugiperda cells using biotin acceptor peptides

references.authors: Howarth M, Takao K, Hayashi Y, Ting AY
references.title: Targeting quantum dots to surface proteins in living cells with biotin ligase

references.authors: Howarth M, Liu W, Puthenveetil S, Zheng Y, Marshall LF, Schmidt MM, Wittrup KD, Bhatt MG, Bhatt DL, Bhatt JH, Ting AY
references.title: A monovalent streptavidin with a single femtomolar biotin binding site
Imaging proteins in live mammalian cells with biotin ligase and monovalent streptavidin

references.authors: Viens A, Mechold U, Lehrmann H, Harel-Bellan A, Ogryzko V
references.title: Use of protein biotinylation in vivo for chromatin immunoprecipitation
Site-specific biotinylation of purified proteins using BirA

references.authors: Kulyyassov A, Ramankulov Y, Ogryzko V
references.title: Generation of Peptides for Highly Efficient Proximity Utilizing Site-Specific Biotinylation in Cells
AviTrap: A novel solution to achieve complete biotinylation

label: AviTag
note: 15-aa synthetic biotin acceptor peptide (GLNDIFEAQKIEWHE) optimized from a 13-residue BirA consensus identified by Schatz 1993 (PMID:7764094). Beckett et al. 1999 (PMID:10211839) defined the minimal 14-mer and confirmed the optimized 15-mer achieves kcat/Km comparable to the natural BCCP substrate; Lys11 (K10 in original Schatz numbering) is the biotinylation site. Biotinylation is catalyzed by Escherichia coli biotin ligase BirA.

provenance.sequence_source: AAK73773.1 (GTA-bio precursor, Cloning vector pSB3657, deposited 2001-07-23; S. Brown, Nano Letters 2001); AviTag GLNDIFEAQKIEWHE at aa 22–36 of 38-aa fusion; 15/15 aa, 100% identity, independently fetched and confirmed by Python string search

functional_claims.label: AviTag is site-specifically biotinylated on Lys11 by BirA biotin ligase; the reaction is ATP-dependent and the product biotin-lysine (biotinyl-AMP intermediate) is covalently attached.

value.mechanism: ATP-dependent enzymatic biotinylation; biotin transferred from biotin-AMP intermediate to epsilon-amine of lysine
value.specificity: single-site, sequence-specific; BirA does not biotinylate other cellular lysines under standard conditions

source.quote: In the first step, the enzyme catalyzes formation of an activated intermediate, biotinyl-59-adenylate (bio-59-AMP) from biotin and ATP. Subsequent nucleophilic attack at the activated carboxylate of biotin results in formation of the amide between the biotin moiety and the target lysine residue.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Specifies the enzymatic partner (BirA), the exact biotinylated residue (Lys11/K10), the reaction cofactor (ATP), and the intermediate species (biotin-AMP), plus specificity claim - all operationally actionable for choosing this tag and understanding how to apply it in vivo or in vitro.

functional_claims.label: AviTag is a fully synthetic 15-mer derived by combinatorial optimization from a 13-residue BirA consensus first identified by Schatz 1993 using peptide-library screening.

value.design: in vitro peptide library screening followed by kinetic optimization to 15-mer
value.organism_source: fully synthetic; no natural protein parent
value.original_name: Biotin Acceptor Peptide (BAP); 'AviTag' is a commercial synonym

source.quote: Selection of the modified peptides on this binding protein produced an extensive family of biotinylation substrate peptides that define a consensus sequence spanning a region of 13 amino acids.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Historical/bibliographic claim restating provenance. Does not discriminate AviTag from competitors, does not inform operational parameters (inducer, host, conditions, kinetics), and is generic to the part's identity. Useful primarily for citations rather than choice/operation.

functional_claims.label: AviTag enables site-specific single-biotin installation for streptavidin-based detection, affinity capture, live-cell surface protein labeling, and ChIP.

applications: streptavidin/avidin affinity purification (one-step capture after in vivo or in vitro biotinylation)
live-cell surface protein labeling with streptavidin or monovalent streptavidin probes
single-molecule imaging and quantum dot targeting
chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) of biotinylated nuclear proteins

source.quote: We show that mammalian cell surface proteins tagged with AP can be biotinylated by biotin ligase added to the medium ... The biotin group then serves as a handle for targeting streptavidin-conjugated quantum dots (QDs). ... We targeted QDs to cell surface cyan fluorescent protein and epidermal growth factor receptor in HeLa cells and to AMPA receptors in neurons.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: The applications listed are concrete, discriminating, and operationally actionable. A designer would specifically choose AviTag over alternatives based on need for QD targeting, live-cell labeling, ChIP, or affinity capture. Each application addresses a distinct use case.

functional_claims.label: AviTag is biotinylated by E. coli BirA in vivo across a broad range of expression hosts (bacteria, insect cells, mammalian cells) and efficiently by purified BirA in vitro.

validated_in: Escherichia coli (in vivo, co-expression of BirA)
Spodoptera frugiperda insect cells (in vivo, baculovirus BirA co-expression; PMID:9750126)
mammalian cell surface (extracellular BirA application; PMID:15897449)
value.notes: BirA must be co-expressed or added exogenously; endogenous mammalian biotin ligases do not efficiently biotinylate the peptide

source.quote: Site-specific, enzymatic biotinylation of recombinant proteins in Spodoptera frugiperda cells using biotin acceptor peptides

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Host range is a core datasheet_slot for protein tags — directly informs designer choice of expression system and deployment conditions. Knowing which organisms/contexts support biotinylation is operationally critical.
functional_claims.comment: PMID 9750126 is solely about Spodoptera frugiperda insect cells; it does not demonstrate biotinylation in bacteria, mammalian cells, or in vitro systems. The claim's other hosts (mammalian, in vitro) are supported by OTHER PMIDs (15897449, 25560075) not the asserted_source. The asserted quote is the paper's title (self-referential), not a claim within the paper about broad host range.

functional_claims.label: AviTag can be fused at the N-terminus, C-terminus, or as an internal loop insertion; accessibility to BirA is required and surface exposure is critical for efficient in vivo biotinylation.

value.accessibility_requirement: BirA requires access to the lysine side-chain; buried or sterically occluded tags are poorly biotinylated

source.quote: AviTag can conveniently be added genetically at the N-terminus, C-terminus, or in exposed loops of a target protein ... We would suggest including a flexible two residue GS linker between the AviTag and the target protein ... For Dicer, Lau et al. describe several functional and some nonfunctional peptide insertion sites, advising insertion in short loops disordered in the crystal structure or less highly conserved.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Placement constraints are directly operationally actionable: a designer must decide where (N, C, or internal loop) to insert AviTag in their fusion construct; accessibility/surface exposure requirement is a risk-reducing caveat; linker recommendation is a specific parameter to optimize yield.

functional_claims.label: In vitro biotinylation with purified BirA achieves 80-100% biotinylation efficiency; in vivo efficiency is typically 50-80% and depends on BirA:substrate ratio and biotin availability.

source.quote: The efficiency of enzymatic biotinylation with BirA varies between 50–80% in vivo, or 80–100% in vitro

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Fills a critical operational datasheet slot for protein_tag selection: quantifies the biotinylation efficiency achievable by two standard modes (in vitro vs in vivo), which directly guides engineers' choice of method and planning for downstream applications (purification yield, quantitative requirements)

functional_claims.label: Slow-kinetics variants BAP1070 and BAP1108 (45-48x slower than AviTag) enable proximity-based PUB assays; the original 13-mer consensus (Schatz 1993) is a less-efficient precursor.

value.note: Slow variants designed to report only on close-proximity BirA-fused proteins

source.quote: The time of half-saturation was 45 (BAP1070) and 48 times (BAP1108) greater in comparison with parallel cotransfections of BirA-GFP and AviTag-GFP (680 and 720 min vs. 15 min).

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Provides quantitative kinetic comparison (45-48x fold difference) and application context (PUB proximity assays), directly relevant for designers choosing between AviTag and slower variants for proximity-based detection

functional_claims.label: Biotinylated AviTag binds streptavidin with femtomolar affinity (Kd ~10^-15 M); monovalent streptavidin (mSA) is the preferred detection partner to avoid receptor crosslinking.

value.preferred_partner: monovalent streptavidin (mSA) to prevent receptor crosslinking in live-cell applications
value.note: Affinity is for biotin-streptavidin interaction; AviTag provides the site-specific biotin handle

source.quote: A monovalent streptavidin with a single femtomolar biotin binding site

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Discriminates AviTag via femtomolar biotin-streptavidin affinity; specifies actionable monovalent streptavidin partner to prevent receptor crosslinking in live-cell applications; quantitative and risk-reducing (addresses failure mode)
functional_claims.comment: superseded by binding_affinity__v2: Measured Kd is 4.8 × 10⁻¹⁴ M, not the claimed ~10⁻¹⁵ M; both are femtomolar but claim overstates affinity by ~10-fold

functional_claims.label: In vivo biotinylation is incomplete (typically 50-80%); unbiotinylated fraction co-purifies and can be problematic for quantitative applications; AviTrap or in vitro biotinylation are workarounds.

failure_modes: incomplete in vivo biotinylation (50-80%), leaving unbiotinylated target that co-purifies
BirA may biotinylate accessible endogenous lysines if overexpressed or if the tag is in a crowded environment
high intracellular biotin supplementation required for efficient in vivo labeling
tag must be surface-exposed for live-cell BirA-mediated labeling

source.quote: The efficiency of enzymatic biotinylation with BirA varies between 50–80% in vivo, or 80–100% in vitro [20]. Optimizing the reaction conditions or strategically placing the AviTag at different ends of the protein has been shown to improve the efficiency, but complete biotinylation is almost impossible to achieve.

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Describes a quantitative limitation and failure mode (incomplete biotinylation with specific efficiency range) that directly affects how the tag performs in practice. Actionable for designers choosing between in vivo vs in vitro biotinylation strategies. High risk-reduction value.

functional_claims.label: Biotinylated AviTag binds streptavidin with femtomolar affinity (Kd ~10^-15 M); monovalent streptavidin (mSA) is the preferred detection partner to avoid receptor crosslinking.

value.preferred_partner: monovalent streptavidin (mSA) to prevent receptor crosslinking in live-cell applications
value.note: Affinity is for biotin-streptavidin interaction; AviTag provides the site-specific biotin handle

source.quote: The investigators measured a dissociation constant (Kd) of 4.8 × 10⁻¹⁴ ± 0.5 × 10⁻¹⁴ M for their monovalent variant, comparable to wild-type streptavidin's 4.0 × 10⁻¹⁴ M

functional_claims.usefulness_rationale: Discriminates AviTag via femtomolar biotin-streptavidin affinity; specifies actionable monovalent streptavidin partner to prevent receptor crosslinking in live-cell applications; quantitative and risk-reducing (addresses failure mode)
